pages.raw_text:
Next Monday at 10 a. m. the
greatest Labor day parade ever wit.
messed in the Inland empire will
form in line on Riverside west OF
Monroe ST. The program has been
completed for celebrating labor's
annual holiday and there is every
indication that the procession will
show at least 5000 union men in
line. Hillyard will send in dele.
cation of 200. No discord has
marred this years preparations for
the celebration.


AS outlined in the official pro,
gram the parade will be led by
Thomas Joyce, marshal Of the day,
and a platoon of police. Following
these will come the speakers of the
day, e. O. Conner and H. L.
Hughes; president Of the day, J.
H. Stevenson, Mayor Moore, or
Acting Mayor Pratt, the city coun.
Gil and city commissioners, all In
carriages.


Each of the divisions Of the
parade will be led by band. The
first section will be made up of the
bricklayers, blacksmiths, machin.
lists, molders, pressmen, clerks, bar
tenders, cooks, walters and wait.
resses and Federal union 1924, In
the order of the age Of their
charters.


The second division will be head.
ed by a platoon of the assistant
marshals OF the day on foot, leading
the building trades. The third di
vision will include the team drivers
on foot; the team owners, in rigs
and Federal union 1929. The lat
ter union expects to have nearly
1000 men in line.


Several floats will accompany
the parade in competition.


From Monroe and Riverside the
parade will march on Riverside to
Sprague, from there to Division, on
Division to. Riverside and back
down to Monroe, where 1t will be
reviewed by the mayor and city
council and the marshal and
aides.


Speaking will begin at Mantle
park about 2 O'Clock and the ath'
letic contests will be held at the
same place during the afternoon.
